title: User Endpoint Patch Latency Beyond SLA

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 A.8.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 A.8.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.6.25: User Endpoint Patch Latency Beyond SLA.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.6.25' running on intune:device_compliance, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=mdm sourcetype=intune:device_compliance earliest=-7d
| where critical_updates_pending>0 AND days_since_contact<3
| stats sum(critical_updates_pending) as pending by device_name, os
description: Tracks missing critical patches on user endpoints to evidence endpoint hardening for user devices.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes mdm and sourcetypes intune:device_compliance. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Define SLA days; (2) auto-ticket top offenders; (3) executive patch scorecard. Indexes required: `index=mdm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=intune:device_compliance`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for tracks missing critical patches on user endpoints to evidence endpoint hardening for user devices. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Microsoft Intune or Tanium.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `intune:device_compliance`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Define SLA days; (2) auto-ticket top offenders; (3) executive patch scorecard.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=mdm sourcetype=intune:device_compliance earliest=-7d
| where critical_updates_pending>0 AND days_since_contact<3
| stats sum(critical_updates_pending) as pending by device_name, os
```

Understanding this SPL

**User Endpoint Patch Latency Beyond SLA (A.8.1)** — Tracks missing critical patches on user endpoints to evidence endpoint hardening for user devices.

Documented **Data sources**: `intune:device_compliance`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Microsoft Intune or Tanium. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: mdm; **sourcetype**: intune:device_compliance. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=mdm, sourcetype=intune:device_compliance, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where critical_updates_pending>0 AND days_since_contact<3` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by device_name, os** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**User Endpoint Patch Latency Beyond SLA (A.8.1)** — Tracks missing critical patches on user endpoints to evidence endpoint hardening for user devices.

Documented **Data sources**: `intune:device_compliance`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Microsoft Intune or Tanium. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Endpoint.Processes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart, Table
